posts.text: Think I'm finally done here. Actually having fun on the sky app like it's 2019 again. See me there, same username 🫶👋

Trump has already established an EPA transition team led by two former fossil fuel lobbyists with “years of experience in dismantling environmental protections” 

I interviewed activists about where we go from here: heated.world/p/trumps-enviro…
My partner works for Wisp. Can confirm this is true. whnt.com/news/americans-stoc…
See, the Trump administration will protect access to clean air and water. Like it won't be widely available but definitely you will be able to access it somewhere

Trump’s re-election is the nail in the coffin for 1.5 degrees Celsius, the critical threshold past which the planet will experience significantly more extreme climate impacts, nine climate scientists and policy experts told us this week. heated.world/p/15c-is-dead-t…

I have a new answer to the question, “What does media need to do now that Trump has been re-elected?” I think you guys are really gonna like it


Rachel Donald (@CrisisReports)

WTF with @emorwee 

What a Trump Presidency means for climate action – with added humour because if you're not laughing you're crying amirite.

Listen to the full episode now: planetcritical.com/p/wtf-emi…

Video
  





— https://nitter.net/CrisisReports/status/1854642865172783524#m
Stepping outside on an 80 degree November day realizing not only that Donald Trump will be president for the next four years, but that this will be the coldest November for the rest of my life

Fighting lawlessness with the law:
“The E.P.A. came out with no new science, no new law or legal precedent that would allow them to walk away from the endangerment finding." www.nytimes.com/2026/03/19/c...
What a great story. You’ll never look at your dog the same way again. (Terrific photos too)   www.nytimes.com/2026/03/18/m...
fossil fuels are a national security threat, you're hearing it more and more
Cops have unlimited ability to ruin our lives and as soon as they get a little pushback they turn into the biggest babies in the world part 100,000
"Puff daddy. Enough already. Now it's P Diddy. Still sounds shitty."
People forget that Afroman is the king of smack talk. His song “whack rappers” is probably the funniest and most wide-ranging diss track of all time

Totally! His climate philanthropy allows HIM to be in control of what climate “solutions” society pursues (AI + others in his business interest) and which solutions it avoids (transitioning from fossil fuels) 

This article on “Philanthrocapitalism” was really eye-opening for me

“Giving to charity is a prime opportunity for CEOs to be seen to be doing good without having to sacrifice their commitment to making profit at any social cost.”

“Charitable activity permits CEOs to be philanthropic rather than economically progressive or politically democratic.”
The primary thing Bezos gets in return for these donations is reputational boost. Philanthropy is a PR strategy.

Bezos is one of the biggest funders of climate orgs but behaves like someone who works for the oil and gas industry. Which makes one wonder what he gets in return for all of those donations. 

 "according to the Bezos Earth Fund, AI could be humanity’s best hope for curbing climate change"

Big Oil is no longer trying to convince us they want to preserve a safe & stable climate

Now, they're focused on convincing us that the world is only safe & stable if they're in charge

That's the thesis of a new @cleancreatives.bsky.social report analyzing 2000 oil ads, exclusively in HEATED today
Jesus. Paying a French company a billion dollars *not* to build a wind farm.
"The oil industry is spending $7 billion a year telling you oil is inevitable not because it’s true, but because it desperately needs everyone to believe it."

"But it’s important to remember: The oil industry is spending $7 billion a year telling you oil is inevitable not because it’s true, but because it desperately needs everyone to believe it. If everyone does not believe this, they are screwed."
Ok but do you guys like the custom illustration I made for this article
Amazing piece of work here:

Great story on new @cleancreatives.bsky.social research showing that the era of fossil fuel greenwashing is largely over.

Ever since the 2022 Ukraine invasion oil and gas companies have doubled down on extraction, and are using ad and PR campaigns to try to crush their opposition not embrace them.

Big Oil is no longer trying to convince us they want to preserve a safe & stable climate

Now, they're focused on convincing us that the world is only safe & stable if they're in charge

That's the thesis of a new @cleancreatives.bsky.social report analyzing 2000 oil ads, exclusively in HEATED today
How fossil fuel ads manipulate us. On today’s podcast, we watch and analyze fossil fuel ads.
Fossil fuel propaganda is evolving. A new analysis of nearly 2,000 fossil fuel ads finds Big Oil has moved from green promises to insisting oil and gas are inevitable. (They're not).
Our reaction to the Steyer interview
Can a billionaire fix California?. Tom Steyer argues he's not a regular billionaire. He's a cool billionaire.
NBC's top climate reporter resigns. In an exclusive interview, veteran NBC meteorologist Chase Cain opens up about burnout, suppression, and why he's going independent.
Climate coverage is shrinking. We're expanding it.
Sam Alito has an oil money problem. Nearly 30 percent of Alito’s individual stock portfolio is directly tied to fossil fuels.

Trump is wiping out all climate regulation. Big Oil may regret it.  (by Julia Kane). For decades, oil majors fought climate regulation. Now, they’re afraid Trump’s extreme rollbacks could leave them on the hook to pay for what they’ve done.
Actually, I do know how to do this. In retrospect, paying attention to polluters may be one of the best ways to understand what’s currently happening in the United States.
I don't know how to do this
Air pollution denial is now EPA policy. Dirty air may kill people, but Trump's EPA won't count the bodies.
RFK Jr. forgot what makes us healthy. Healthy food is not healthy if it destroys the environment that produces it.
It's time to embrace climate conspiracy . Trump’s Venezuela oil play exposes what climate reporting has documented for decades—if we’re willing to say it out loud.
Exxon’s new greenwashing ploy (by Emily Sanders). The oil giant claims it can eliminate more than 90 percent of emissions from gas-powered AI facilities. Critics say that’s nonsense.
The anti-Teflon chef. Andrew Zimmern talks about why he supported California’s proposed PTFE phase-out—and what he thinks of the celebrity chefs who opposed it.
